source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure SSH is restricted to only IP address that should have this access.

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure SSH is restricted to only IP address that should have this access.
input_row.description: Any ports enable within Lightsail by default are open and exposed to the world. For SSH and RDP access you should identify which IP address need access.
input_row.rationale: Any ports enable within Lightsail by default are open and exposed to the world. This can result in outside traffic trying to access or even deny access to the Lightsail instances. Removing and adding approved IP address required for access.
input_row.audit: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Linux Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Confirm that the SSH Port is restricted to an IP address Application Protocol Port or range / Code Restricted to SSH  TCP  22   101.221.11.11 7. If SSH is needed and it is open to Any IPv4 address refer to the remediation below. From the Command Line: 1. Run aws lightsail get-instances aws lightsail get-instances --query "instances[*].name" This command will provide a list of Instance names. "WordPress-1", "Windows_Server_2019-1"  2. Run aws lightsail get-instance-port-states for any Linux instances listed aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name> This command will provide a list of available Ports for the Instance name. { "fromPort": 22, "toPort": 22, "protocol": "tcp", "state": "open", "cidrs": [ "0.0.0.0/0" "101.221.11.11/32" ], "cidrListAliases": [] }, 3. Review the Port 22 settings and confirm that the only IP Addresses that should have access to the instance are listed in the cidrs as shown above. 4. If it is open to all ports (0.0.0.0/0) of there is an IP address listed that shouldn't have access refer to the remediation below.
input_row.remediation: From the Console: 1. Login to AWS Console using https://console.aws.amazon.com 2. Click All services, click Lightsail under Compute. 3. This will open up the Lightsail console. 4. Select the Linux Instance you want to review. 5. Go to the Networking section. 6. Under IPv4 networking find the SSH rule as shown below. Application Protocol Port or range / Code Restricted to SSH  TCP  22   Any IPv4 address  7. Click on the edit icon 8. Click on the check box next to Restrict to IP address 9. Under Source IP address (192.0.2.0) or range (192.0.2.0- 192.0.2.255 or 192.0.2.0/24) type the IP address' you want. From the Command Line: 1. Run aws lightsail put-ins aws lightsail put-instance-public-ports --instance-name <instance_name> -- port-info fromPort=22,protocol=TCP,toPort=22,cidrs=110.111.221.100/32,110.111.221.202/3 2 This command will enter the IP addresses that should have access to the instances identified above in the Audit. 2. Run aws lightsail get-instance-port-states for the Linux instance to confirm the new setting. aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name> This command will provide a list of available Ports and show how the cidr value for Port 22 is now set. { "fromPort": 22, "toPort": 22, "protocol": "tcp", "state": "open", "cidrs": [ "110.111.221.100/32", "110.111.221.202/32" ], "cidrListAliases": [] },  3. Repeat the remediation below for all other instances identified in the Audit. References: 1. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/lightsail/ind ex.html#cli-aws-lightsail

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The compliance control can be verified using AWS APIs and AWS CLI commands. The control checks if SSH access to AWS Lightsail instances is restricted to specific IP addresses instead of being open to all. This check is based on retrieving and inspecting the port configuration of Lightsail instances, which is exposed through AWS APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_lightsail_ssh_access_ip_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Calls/Boto3 SDK Methods to Use:** 
  - Use `boto3.client('lightsail').get_instances()` to list all Lightsail instances.
  - For each instance, use `boto3.client('lightsail').get_instance_port_states(InstanceName=<instance_name>)` to retrieve the port configuration.
  
- **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
  - `aws lightsail get-instances` to enumerate all instances.
  - `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name>` to check the port states for SSH specifically.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Lightsail instances across available regions.
  
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Check that port 22 (SSH) is not exposed to `0.0.0.0/0`.
  - Validate that only approved IP addresses are listed in the `cidrs` property for port 22.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass:** Port 22 is restricted to specific IP addresses (e.g., `101.221.11.11/32`) and not open to `0.0.0.0/0`.
  - **Fail:** Port 22 is open to `0.0.0.0/0`, indicating unrestricted SSH access.
  
- **Example:**
  - Use the CLI command `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name>` and check the JSON output for entries such as `fromPort: 22, state: open, cidrs: ["0.0.0.0/0"]`. If any instance has this configuration, it should be flagged as non-compliant.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. This control can be effectively checked using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, which allows for programmatic access to the configuration of AWS Lightsail instances.
- AWS SDK capabilities and AWS CLI commands are well-suited for this task, as they provide the necessary methods to retrieve and inspect the port configurations of Lightsail instances.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of `boto3.client('lightsail').get_instances()` and `boto3.client('lightsail').get_instance_port_states(InstanceName=<instance_name>)` is appropriate for retrieving instance information and port configurations.
- The AWS CLI commands `aws lightsail get-instances` and `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name>` are valid and correctly specified for this task.
- The program name `aws_lightsail_ssh_access_ip_restricted` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, although it could be slightly improved for clarity.
- The pass/fail criteria are clear and align with the control requirements.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_lightsail_instance_ssh_ip_restriction_check` for improved clarity and consistency with AWS resource naming conventions.
- **Additional Considerations:** The assessment could include a check for other ports that might be used for administrative access (e.g., RDP on port 3389) to ensure comprehensive coverage of remote access controls.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules, such as custom rules, to continuously monitor and alert on non-compliant configurations. This would enhance the automation by providing real-time compliance checks.
- **Documentation:** Provide examples of approved IP address formats and how they should be configured for clarity.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured, and the use of AWS APIs and CLI commands is appropriate for the control being audited. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is sound.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_lightsail_instance_ssh_ip_restriction_check` for improved clarity.
- Consider expanding the scope to include checks for other administrative ports and explore the use of AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS Lightsail exposes port configuration details—including SSH access rules—via the AWS API and CLI, specifically through the boto3 `get_instance_port_states` method and equivalent CLI commands. This allows for programmatic, scalable, and reliable auditing of whether SSH (port 22) is restricted to specific IP addresses, without requiring manual console inspection or interactive instance access.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_lightsail_instance_ssh_ip_restriction_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: Lightsail Instances
- API/CLI Method:
  - boto3: `lightsail.get_instances()`, `lightsail.get_instance_port_states(InstanceName=<instance_name>)`
  - AWS CLI: `aws lightsail get-instances`, `aws lightsail get-instance-port-states --instance-name <instance_name>`
- Properties to Check:
  - For each instance, inspect the port info for port 22 (SSH) in the returned port states.
  - Check the `cidrs` property for port 22 rules.
- Pass Criteria:
  - Port 22 (SSH) is NOT open to `0.0.0.0/0` or `::/0`.
  - Port 22 is restricted to one or more specific, approved IP addresses or CIDR blocks (e.g., `101.221.11.11/32`).
- Fail Criteria:
  - Port 22 (SSH) is open to `0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`, or any rule that allows unrestricted access from any IPv4 or IPv6 address.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There was no substantive disagreement; both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a slightly more descriptive program name, which has been adopted for clarity and alignment with AWS resource naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
